items.title: 'Iranians succeeded in making Strait of Hormuz primary issue rather than their nuclear programme'
items.description: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, Professor of International Politics at the University College Dublin. Lucas argues that the centre of gravity has fundamentally shifted. And he highlights the growing divergence between American diplomatic objectives, Israeli territorial ambitions, and Iranian efforts to secure economic recovery after a devastating conflict. Rather than depicting the current ceasefire as a pathway towards peace, Lucas characterises it as an unstable equilibrium sustained by mutual restraint, unresolved ambitions, and competing visions of regional order.

items.title: 'If Ukraine falls, this will be the end of the European Union and the end of Europe's future'
items.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Volodymyr Yermolenko, Ukrainian philosopher, journalist, author and President of PEN Ukraine. According to Dr Yermolenko, the war is not simply a military confrontation, Europe's future hangs in the balance. Speaking hours after a devastating missile attack on Kyiv, he juxtaposes the intimate experience of civilian vulnerability with a broader strategic analysis, arguing that Russia's continued attacks on residential areas reflect military frustration rather than momentum. He contends that Ukraine's comparative advantage lies in asymmetric warfare, technological innovation, and the mobilisation of civil society, while warning that European support is not merely an act of solidarity but a matter of continental self-preservation.

items.description: Diego Santilli formally took over as President Javier Milei's Cabinet chief on Tuesday, completing a transition triggered by Manuel Adorni's resignation amid a corruption investigation. Leer más

items.title: How haircuts are helping men recover at a Kenyan mental health hospital
items.description: At Kenya’s largest mental health referral hospital, male patients are offered a different kind of therapy: a personal grooming session provided by two visiting barbers. Healthcare workers say personal grooming is an important aspect of recovery, as patients often lose interest in self-care.

items.title: Australian Prime Minister condemns delay of changes to child social media ban
items.description: Parliament passed the initial legislation with overwhelming support in 2024

items.title: 'No Israeli security without Palestinian freedom; no Palestinian freedom without Israeli security'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Dr. Gershon Baskin, Middle East Director of the International Communities Organisation and former hostage negotiator. One thousand days after the outbreak of the Gaza war, Gershon Baskin shares a stark assessment of Israel's political and strategic trajectory. Rather than framing the anniversary solely as a military milestone, he argues that it represents a profound failure of political leadership, strategic planning and institutional accountability.

items.title: Russia launches attacks on Kyiv, killing at least 27 and injuring scores
items.description: Ballistic and cruise missiles and drones were used in what Kyiv's mayor, Vitali Klitschko, said was the largest attack on the city since the war began.

items.title: NATO leaders to gather in Ankara, aiming to smooth over tensions with Trump
items.description: “NATO is, and will always be, a transatlantic alliance but we need to rebalance it for the better,” NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said

items.title: Buenos Aires City poverty rate rises after five quarters of decline
items.description: Over 651,000 porteños – more than one in five residents – were living in poverty in the first quarter of 2026, according to the IDECBA city statistics bureau. Leer más

items.description: Military command issues threat a day after Qatari mediators hailed 'positive progress' in indirect US-Iranian talks.

items.title: Prosecutors awaiting files relating to woman found dead in Brisbane River
items.description: Jesse Beale, 49, is charged over the death of his former partner, Crystal Beale, and misconduct with her corpse.

items.title: Thailand denies viral claim Macron knelt before king

items.title: Man holding Tibetan flag dies after setting himself on fire outside UN
items.description: Man described by campaigner as 'a tireless advocate for Tibet' pronounced dead on arrival at hospital.

items.title: Adorni resigns, blames media and clears path for Santilli
items.description: The political death throes of Javier Milei's third Cabinet chief lasted longer than expected. There is relief in the Casa Rosada after a pincer movement involving all libertarian factions, PRO, Patricia Bullrich and the UCR. Diego Santilli is preparing to lead the cabinet and repair the damage. Leer más

items.title: Trips, wealth and property – The judicial cases closing in on Manuel Adorni
items.description: Investigators have ordered a forensic review of the former Cabinet chief's finances and are examining the circumstances of his private jet trip to Uruguay and real-estate investments. Leer más

items.title: Springborg hoses down speculation he will be Qld's next governor
items.description: Lawrence Springborg says he has not asked for, sought, nor been offered the role of governor, who represents King Charles in Queensland.

items.title: In Sudan's Kordofan, a key city reels as paramilitary offensive looms
items.description: In a displacement camp near El-Obeid in Sudan's southern Kordofan region, Agsam Hamad trudges through searing heat to fetch murky water from a distant well, as paramilitary forces unleash their fiercest assault yet on the strategic city.

items.title: Russian strikes kill 21 in biggest ever attack on Kyiv, mayor says
items.description: “Kyiv is under attack from ballistic missiles and UAVs,” Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said on Telegram, adding that blasts could be heard across the city.

items.title: Argentina’s beef export boom to United States leaves locals paying more for steak
items.description: Argentina’s exports to the United States are expanding fast past premium steaks to lower-quality cuts. That's great for exporters, but worse for locals, who see prices becoming less affordable. Leer más

South African police say anti-immigrant protests were 'mostly peaceful'
items.description: South African police said Wednesday that nationwide anti-immigration protests held the previous day were "mostly peaceful", despite isolated incidents of looting and criminal activity.

items.title: Germany: Heat wave is over, political fallout has just begun
items.description: After a major heat wave, critics say leaders need to do more for climate change adaption. Policymakers disagree on who's responsible to prepare Germany for extreme temperatures ― the federal government or municipalities.

items.title: EU slaps €3 duty on low-value imported packages
items.description: The EU has moved to curb what it sees as unfair competition from mostly Chinese online retailers by imposing its levy. It follows a similar move from the US.

items.title: At least 16 arrested as Niger expands anti-LGBTQ measures
items.description: Nigerian authorities have arrested at least 16 people following the introduction of harsh new penalties for same-sex sexual relations, marking a significant escalation in the country's stance on LGBTQ rights.

items.title: Residents of Ethiopian town forced to kill hundreds of their own dogs after rabies deaths
items.description: Some residents reluctantly hung their dogs to avoid being arrested or fined, locals tell the BBC.

items.title: UN warns likelihood of ‘extreme weather events’ as El Nino set to intensify
items.description: World Meteorological Organization forecasts more likelihood of heatwaves, droughts and heavy rainfall due to El Nino.

items.title: China says pilot crashed small plane into skyscraper for 'personal reasons'
items.description: The 66-year-old, who died in the crash, had anxiety and referenced "ending his life" in his diary.

items.title: Argentina's economy contracted in April as uneven growth persists
items.description: Economic activity fell 1.5% from March; From a year ago, gross domestic product proxy grew 1.6%. Leer más

items.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths in June heatwave
items.description: France recorded 2,025 excess deaths during the June 22-28 heatwave, the country's health minister said Friday, marking a nearly 30 percent nationwide rise in recorded deaths. In the Paris region, the number of deaths during the same period increased by 62 percent, according to the public health authority.

Does football need FIFA and its World Cup?
items.description: Though the popularity of the World Cup continues to grow, its organizing body, FIFA, has arguably never been less popular. So why does football still need FIFA? And is there any way out?

items.title: 125 passengers, crew hit with highly contagious stomach virus on cruise from San Francisco
items.description: Ruby Princess was on a 20-day round trip journey from San Francisco to Canada and Alaska when the passengers and crew members were stricken by norovirus, the U.S. Centres for Disease Control and Prevention said

items.title: Star-spangled blowup? Divided United States commemorates 250th anniversary
items.description: The world’s longest-running democracy is celebrating its 250th anniversary. Quite the milestone for a United States that to many Europeans still seems like a new world. Since 1789, it’s been governed under the same constitution, a timespan where here in France, we’ve had five republics, three monarchies and three dictatorships if you include Nazi occupation. So what is the significance of this Fourth of July?

items.title: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
items.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.

items.title: South Africa and Ghana in diplomatic row over alleged killing of migrant
items.description: South Africa denies claims that a Ghanaian citizen was killed in anti-migrant protests in Cape Town.

items.title: Kyiv mourns 30 killed in Russian attack as rescuers continue search
items.description: Rescuers continued ⁠clearing the rubble ​in Kyiv on Friday in the hope of finding more survivors, as flags were ​lowered to half mast to mark a day of mourning, a day after a Russian missile and drone attack killed at least 30 people. France 24's Emmanuelle Chaze reports from the Ukrainian capital.

items.title: Algerians vote for new parliament with turnout in focus
items.description: Algerians headed to the polls on Thursday to elect a new parliament in an election shadowed by expectations of low turnout among the 25-million-person electorate
